LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BROADMOOR POLICE PROTECTION DISTRICT, JOSEPH SHERIDAN, RALPH HUTCHENS, DAVID PARENTI, CHARLES SMITH AND ARTHUR STELLINI'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS: 1) OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF SECTION 1983 AGAINST ALL DEFENDANTS; 2) SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF SECTION 1983 AGAINST THE DISTRICT; 3) SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF SECTION 1983 AGAINST ALL INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS; 4) SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE RALPH ACT AGAINST ALL DEFENDANTS; AND 5) SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE NINTH AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FALSE LIGHT AND DEFAMATION AGAINST ALL DEFENDANTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BROADMOOR POLICE PROTECTION DISTRICT, JOSEPH SHERIDAN, RALPH HUTCHENS, DAVID PARENTI, CHARLES SMITH AND ARTHUR STELLINI'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AS TO ALL PORTIONS OF THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE BANE ACT OTHER THAN THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING FALSE ARREST AND IS DENIED AS TO THE ELEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CIVIL CONSPIRACY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

Attys. WALTER H. WALKER, ESQ. and BEAU R. BURBIDGE, ESQ., and Law Clerk JEFF WALKER of Walker, Hamlton, Koenig & Burbidge, LLP (Tel: 415-986-3339) appearing with Plaintiff J. WAYNE JOHNSON;
Atty. MARK R. VELASQUEZ of Hunt Jeppson & Griffin, LLP (Tel: 916-780-7008) appearing with Defendants JOSEPH SHERIDAN, RALPH HUTCHENS, DAVID PARENTI, and ARTHUR STELLINI, and on behalf of Defendant Charles Smith.

The Court conducts an unreported mandatory settlement conference with counsel for parties and parties. The parties reach a tentative settlement. Judge: Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (305/MEW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-17-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Beau R. Burbidge, of Walker, Hamilton, Koenig & Burbidge, LLP, Tel. 415.986.3339, for plaintiffs;
Mark R. Velasquez, of Hunt Jeppson & Griffin LLP, Tel. 916.780.7008, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling in its entirety. DEFENDANT BROADMOOR POLICE PROTECTION DISTRICT, JOSEPH SHERIDAN, RALPH HUTCHENS, DAVID PARENTI, CHARLES SMITH, ARTHUR STELLINI MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied and their alternative motion for summary adjudication is: a) denied as to the first, second, fifth, seventh, eighth and ninth causes of action, b) granted as to the third cause of action as to Mr. Sheridan and Mr. Hutchens only, and c) denied as to the third cause of action as to all defendants other than Mr. Sheridan and Mr. Hutchens. Order signed in open court. (See full Word Minutes and/or order for complete ruling). Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Continued To Aug-24-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion Of Defs. Mtn To Strike Sac
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BROADMOOR POLICE PROTECTION DISTRICT, JOSEPH SHERIDAN, RALPH HUTCHENS, DAVID PARENTI, CHARLES SMITH and ARTHUR STELLINI'S Motion To Strike Second Amended Complaint is granted as to all portions of the fifth cause of action for violation of the Bane Act other than the allegations regarding false arrest and is denied as to the eleventh cause of action for civil conspiracy. Other than the allegations regarding false arrest, the allegations of wrongdoing in the fifth cause of action are barred by Government Code 820.2 and 821.6. While conspiracy is not technically a cause of action, it is common practice and not prejudicial to defendants for conspiracy to be alleged as a separate claim. To strike the eleventh cause of action and then permit plaintiff to allege conspiracy in the general allegations of his complaint serves no useful purpose. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BROADMOOR POLICE PROTECTION DISTRICT, JOSEPH SHERIDAN, RALPH HUTCHENS, DAVID PARENTI, CHARLES SMITH and ARTHUR STELLINI'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is: 1) overruled as to the first, second, and third causes of action for violation of section 1983 against all defendants; 2) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for violation of section 1983 against the District; 3) sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for violation of section 1983 against all individual defendants; 4) sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for violation of the Ralph Act against all defendants; and 5) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the ninth and tenth causes of action for false light and defamation against all defendants. Sheridan and Hutchens are not entitled to absolute immunity for the first, second and third causes of action because those claims are based in part on the alleged false arrest and unconstitutional search and seizure of plaintiff and his property. False arrest and unconstitutional search and seizure are not legislative activities. Defendants are not entitled to qualified immunity for the first, second and third causes of action because those claims are based in part on clearly proscribed unconstitutional actions which all reasonable government officers know are prohibited. The fourth cause of action for municipal liability fails to specify an official government policy or custom for which the District can be held liable under section 1983. Plaintiff is given leave to correct this deficiency if he can do so in good faith. The fourth cause of action makes no sense against the individual defendants. Municipal liability is a basis to seek relief against a government entity, not a government officer. The sixth cause of action for violation of the Ralph Act fails as a matter of law against all defendants because it is premised on: 1) alleged discrimination based on protected characteristics which are not mentioned in plaintiff's government tort claim and 2) alleged violence or intimidation by violence consisting only of an arrest, which does not support a Ralph Act claim. Plaintiff effectively acknowledges that his claims for false light and defamation as now alleged are inadequately pled and barred by the litigation privilege. Plaintiff is given leave to allege cognizable claims for defamation and false light that are not barred by the litigation privilege if he can do so in good faith. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. BEAU R. BURBIDGE, ESQ. of Walker Hamilton & Koenig, Tel No. 415-986-3339 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. MARK R. VELASQUEZ, ESQ. of Hunt Jeppson & Griffin LLP, Tel No. 916-780-7008 appearing for the Defendants Broadmoor Police Protection District, Joseph Sheridan, Ralph Hutchens, David Parenti, Charles Smith, and Arthur Stellini.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS BROADMOOR POLICE PROTECTION DISTRICT, JOSEPH SHERIDAN, RALPH HUTCHENS, DAVID PARENTI, CHARLES SMITH, AND ARTHUR STELLINI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, Defendants' demurrer is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all six causes of action alleged against Sheridan and Hutchens, overruled as to the first and fifth causes of action alleged against the Protection District, Parenti, Smith and Stellini and sustained without leave to amend as to the third and fourth causes of action allege against the Protection District, Parenti, Smith and Stellini. Although defendants' request for judicial notice is granted, there is insufficient basis to rule as a matter of law that the search warrant was valid. None of the causes of action alleged against Sheridan and Hutchens are necessarily barred by either Government Code 821.6 or 820.2 because those individuals do not hold positions whose duties would normally not include carrying out police activities such as making an arrest, searching a home and seizing property. However, each of the causes of action alleged Sheridan and Hutchens, as currently pled, is insufficient to state a claim since there is no allegation what part, if any, they played in the arrest of the plaintiff, the search of his homes, and the seizure of his property or what other action or omission they took as to plaintiff for which plaintiff seeks to impose liability on them. As to the police defendants, the first and fifth causes of action are not barred by Government Code 821.6 or 820.2 because those claims allege liability based at least in part on the alleged false arrest of plaintiff, while the third and fourth causes of action are barred by section 821.6 because the alleged trespass and conversion were carried out as part of a police investigation. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Amended Motion To Transfer This Action And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BEAU R. BURBIDGE OF WHK LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-986-3339 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRIAN E, HAMILTON OF HJG LLP, TEL. NO. 1-916-780-7008 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT BROADMOOR POLICE PROTECTION DISTRICT Amended Motion To Transfer This Action And For Sanctions.- Defendant's Motion for Change of Venue to San Mateo County is DENIED. Plaintiff's sixth cause of action is for negligence. Plaintiff does not allege however, that this cause of action is "based on negligent injury to a person or property by the local agency . . . ." (Code of Civil Procedure 394, subd. (a). It is relevant but not dispositive, furthermore, that the gravamen of Plaintiff's lawsuit is in intentional torts. Defendant is correct in that the mandatory provisions of section 394's negligence rule supersede the general provisions of section 395, however plaintiff's cause of action for negligence does not allege specific injuries. Although Plaintiff's cause of action for negligence incorporates allegations for conversion and false arrest, that alone does not establish a threshold showing of "negligent injury to a person or property." (Ibid.). Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
